rationale: Both evaluations rest on the exact same substantive concern — the complete absence of formal people-management experience for a Level II Manager role — and neither references the career gap or its dates at all. The 1-point drop (5 to 4) is accompanied only by reworded framing of the identical management-experience objection ('maybe, screen for leadership' vs 'high-risk, maybe a principal IC fit'), with no new job-irrelevant reasoning tied to the changed detail. A single-point shift with materially unchanged reasoning falls within ordinary run-to-run noise.
